title: Codex OAuth Eve Model Provider Tasks
principles: Prove the direct Codex Responses runtime path before replacing the current AI Gateway Eve model.
Keep Codex OAuth separate from app login, WorkOS, Better Auth, channels, and Eve tool contracts.
Use Effect Schema, tagged errors, Context services, Layers, Config, and KeyValueStore for every runtime-owned boundary.
Use Effect v4 local import paths for KeyValueStore while documenting the public Effect platform concept.
Treat OpenClaw as prior art only; do not adopt OpenClaw packages, code, state files, or gateway behavior.
Never commit, print, snapshot, or persist raw token values outside the approved token store.
globalVerification: Keep the completed personal executor DeepWiki, Parallel Task, and Goose findings in the spec current before implementation starts.
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth test when the package exists
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth build when the package exists
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy test when the app exists
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy build when the app exists
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy smoke-test when the app exists
bun run --filter @bundjil/agent test when app wiring changes
bun run --filter @bundjil/agent build when app wiring changes
Opt-in live proof only with local/private credentials and sanitized output
Vercel preview deployment proof before production deployment when apps/codex-proxy changes

implementationImprovementAuditCounter.description: Before accepting each task, run parent audit passes for ownership/call graph, Effect implementation quality, and verification coverage. Require more passes if Codex auth support, token storage, Eve model-provider wiring, or secret handling remains weak.

tasks.title: Complete executor and DeepWiki research
tasks.description: Authorize executor personal, query DeepWiki for Codex OAuth, Eve model-provider wiring, and effect-smol KeyValueStore, then revise the spec with confirmed findings before code starts.
implementationNotes: Current attempt on 2026-07-07 is blocked on executor-personal OAuth authorization; `codex mcp login executor-personal` opened the browser authorization URL but timed out waiting for the loopback callback.

After the forked thread and successful executor-personal OAuth, `mcp__executor_personal.skills({ name: "execute" })` worked and DeepWiki was discovered at `deepwiki_mcp.user.personaldeepwikimcp.ask_question`.

DeepWiki on `vercel/eve` confirmed `agent.ts` can use a Gateway model string or any AI SDK `LanguageModel`; direct `LanguageModel` providers bypass Gateway attribution/caching and route through their own provider/modelId.

DeepWiki on `openai/codex` confirmed Codex auth modes include ChatGPT OAuth and access tokens, but `ChatgptAuthTokens` are marked unstable/internal. Current evidence does not support using Codex OAuth as arbitrary Eve chat-completion access.

DeepWiki on `Effect-TS/effect` confirmed the KeyValueStore concept, memory/file layers, schema-backed storage, and typed error mapping, while local Effect v4 source remains authoritative for the `effect/unstable/persistence/KeyValueStore` import path.

Parallel Task run `trun_e43fa758cfba40c3bf5ad5cddb1b7196` corrected the implementation direction: subscription-backed model access is viable through a Codex-specific runtime path or pinned OpenAI-compatible bridge, not by treating OAuth tokens as OpenAI API keys.
The full Parallel AI report is committed at docs/product-specs/codex-oauth-subscription-model-access.parallel-research.md.

Executor personal DeepWiki on `aaif-goose/goose` confirmed Goose's `chatgpt_codex` provider uses direct HTTP to `https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses`, not app-server, ACP, or a Codex CLI subprocess.

Executor personal DeepWiki on `Effect-TS/effect` confirmed Effect HTTP API can expose web-standard handlers through HttpRouter/HttpApi layers, with typed JSON endpoints via HttpApi and raw streaming routes via HttpRouter/HttpServerResponse.
Mobius `apps/api` was inspected as the local Vercel deployment reference: app entrypoints own Nitro/Vercel/local dev, packages own Effect HTTP route layers and service composition.
The spec now treats a private Bundjil OpenAI-compatible proxy over direct Codex Responses as the preferred proof target and keeps Eve on AI Gateway until that path is proven.
mandatoryVerification: Executor personal OAuth is authorized or a fresh blocker is recorded with command output.
DeepWiki evidence is added to docs/product-specs/codex-oauth-eve-model-provider.md.
Flat meaningful Effect.gen programs are explicitly required for later primary operations.
Typed errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError are explicitly required.
Canonical Effect Schema contracts and schema-derived types from owning packages are explicitly required.
No unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, or trivial wrappers/helpers are approved by the revised spec.
Implementation-improvement audit pass evidence is recorded before task acceptance.
completionCriteria: The spec names the chosen first runtime path or explicitly blocks implementation if no supported path exists.
The spec distinguishes official OpenAI/Codex support from OpenClaw prior art.
The task ledger is updated with research evidence and audit notes.

evidence: Pass 1 - ownership/call graph: DeepWiki, Parallel, Goose, and Mobius API evidence kept implementation gated. Eve remains app-owned and Gateway-backed by default; direct Codex Responses services live in @bundjil/codex-oauth and Vercel deployment lives in apps/codex-proxy.

Pass 2 - Effect implementation quality: Research task tightened future implementation requirements around Effect KeyValueStore, Effect HTTP API/HttpRouter, Schema-backed profiles, direct Codex HTTP services, request/stream mappers, tagged errors, local v4 import paths, and no API-key fallback without adding code.

Pass 3 - verification coverage: Executor personal OAuth succeeded in the forked thread, DeepWiki returned results for `vercel/eve`, `openai/codex`, `Effect-TS/effect`, and `aaif-goose/goose`, Parallel Task returned Codex subscription findings, Mobius API deployment references were inspected, and the spec/task ledger were updated with the findings.

tasks.title: Define @bundjil/codex-oauth schemas and profile services
tasks.description: Create the package contract with canonical schemas, tagged errors, profile storage, OAuth service tags, storage keys, and memory layers without provider network calls.
implementationNotes: Use package layout src/schemas.ts, src/errors.ts, src/storage-keys.ts, src/profile-store.service.ts, src/oauth.service.ts, src/oauth-client.service.ts, src/live.layer.ts, src/mock.layer.ts, and src/index.ts.
Use effect/unstable/persistence/KeyValueStore for local Effect v4.
Define CodexProfileStore, CodexOAuthService, and CodexOAuthClient as the first stable service tags.
Use hashed subject keys under bundjil/oauth/v1/provider/codex/profile/{subjectHash}.
Do not add live OAuth endpoint exchange, Codex Responses HTTP calls, or Eve model replacement in this task.

Implemented packages/codex-oauth with Effect Schema contracts, per-class tagged errors, storage-key derivation, CodexProfileStore, CodexOAuthService, CodexOAuthClient, KeyValueStore-backed live layer, memory/mock layers, tests, README, and lockfile registration.
Root package export stays contract-focused; live and mock layers are exposed from explicit ./live.layer and ./mock.layer subpaths.
mandatoryVerification: Flat meaningful Effect.gen programs for primary operations.
Typed errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError where relevant.
Canonical Effect Schema contracts and schema-derived types from @bundjil/codex-oauth.
No unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, or trivial wrappers/helpers.
Tests cover schema decode/encode, stable key derivation, missing profile, expired token, remove/revoke, storage error mapping, memory layer seeding, and no token leakage in error messages.
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth test
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth build
bun run check-types
completionCriteria: @bundjil/codex-oauth exports schemas, errors, profile/OAuth service tags, and memory/live layers.
Package README documents contracts, layers, and safe secret handling.
No provider network dependency or Eve app model change is introduced.

evidence: Pass 1 - ownership/call graph: @bundjil/codex-oauth owns Codex OAuth schemas, safe tagged errors, subject-key derivation, profile storage, OAuth service tags, and layers. No apps/agent, apps/codex-proxy, live OAuth endpoint exchange, Codex Responses HTTP calls, or Eve model replacement were introduced.

Pass 2 - Effect implementation quality: primary service operations use flat named Effect.fn / Effect.gen flow, Context.Service tags, Schema-derived types, Schema.RedactedFromValue token fields, per-class Schema.TaggedErrorClass errors, KeyValueStore.toSchemaStore, catchTag/mapError error mapping, and explicit live/mock layer subpaths. Parent scans found no unsafe casts, Object.values/Object.entries, switch, process.env, OPENAI_API_KEY, CODEX_ACCESS_TOKEN, console.log, or real token patterns in package source.

Pass 3 - verification coverage: bun install --frozen-lockfile passed; bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth test passed with 8 tests; bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth build passed; bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth check-types passed; bun run check-types passed across 5 packages; bun run verification passed across lint, knip, typechecks, and tests.

tasks.title: Prove the direct Codex Responses subscription path
tasks.description: Run an opt-in local proof that Codex subscription auth can produce a model result through the direct Codex Responses backend before the private Bundjil proxy task, without exposing secrets.
implementationNotes: Use Goose's `chatgpt_codex` provider as architecture precedent only; do not copy its implementation.
Implement or spike PKCE OAuth against the researched OpenAI issuer/client/scopes behind CodexOAuthService and CodexOAuthClient.
POST a minimal sanitized request to `https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses` through CodexHttpClient.
When subscription auth is selected, do not read or forward OPENAI_API_KEY and assert this in tests.
Private OpenAI-compatible bridge auth remains in `create-codex-proxy-app`; this proof task only proves the direct package-owned Codex Responses backend path.
If an AI SDK LanguageModel over the private proxy is practical, prove generateText with a minimal prompt and sanitized output.
If no supported path exists, mark this task blocked and do not proceed to Eve replacement.
Implemented CodexResponsesFetch, CodexHttpClient, CodexResponsesProof, direct Responses schemas, safe HTTP/request/stream errors, live/mock fetch layers, and `proof:codex-responses`.

Live proof used the local Codex auth cache access token injected into CODEX_ACCESS_TOKEN for one process. The command returned HTTP 200 from `https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses`, 5528 response bytes, 18 stream lines, and `usedAccountHeader: true` without printing tokens, prompts, auth codes, raw OAuth responses, or response bodies.

Executor Personal CLI login completed, but the in-thread `mcp__executor_personal.skills({ name: "execute" })` call still returned `OAuth authorization required`; implementation used prior DeepWiki/Parallel evidence plus current Goose source inspection.
mandatoryVerification: Flat meaningful Effect.gen programs for primary runtime operations.
Typed errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError.
Canonical Effect Schema contracts and schema-derived types from @bundjil/codex-oauth.
No unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, or trivial wrappers/helpers.
OAuth token refresh, Codex Responses request mapping, stream shape proof, no API-key fallback, and failure mapping are tested without network where possible.
Private proxy auth remains explicitly deferred to `create-codex-proxy-app`.
Live proof logs contain no access tokens, refresh tokens, authorization codes, raw OAuth responses, private prompts, or full request/response bodies.
Provider errors are mapped to safe tagged errors.
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth test
bun run check-types
completionCriteria: A sanitized live proof records the direct Codex Responses runtime path.
The spec and package README are updated with the result.
A failure to prove support blocks Eve model replacement rather than adding a fallback fake.

evidence: Pass 1 - ownership/call graph: @bundjil/codex-oauth owns only the direct proof boundary: CodexResponsesFetch -> CodexHttpClient -> CodexResponsesProof. apps/agent and apps/codex-proxy were not changed; Eve still uses AI Gateway until later proxy/provider tasks pass.

Pass 2 - Effect implementation quality: proof operations use Effect Schema contracts, Context.Service tags, Layer composition, Config.redacted/Config.schema with ConfigProvider.fromEnv, Schema.RedactedFromValue token handling, safe Schema.TaggedErrorClass errors, flat Effect.gen operations, and injected fetch layers for tests. The implementation does not read OPENAI_API_KEY or emit raw response bodies.

Pass 3 - verification coverage: mocked tests cover refreshAccessToken, direct request headers/body, chatgpt-account-id behavior, HTTP status mapping without body reads, fetch failure mapping, and no OPENAI_API_KEY fallback. Live proof returned sanitized HTTP 200 metadata from the direct Codex Responses endpoint. Package test, check-types, build, and root `bun run verification` passed.

tasks.description: Create the package contract for mapping OpenAI-compatible requests to Codex Responses and exposing a private proxy without wiring Eve's default model yet.
implementationNotes: Use package layout src/schemas.ts, src/errors.ts, src/codex-http-client.service.ts, src/codex-direct-provider.service.ts, src/codex-request-mapper.ts, src/codex-stream-mapper.ts, src/openai-compatible-proxy.service.ts, and src/index.ts.
Model auth strategies explicitly: subscription profile, test/mock, and future BYOK fallback only after a separate spec update.
Subscription mode must ignore OPENAI_API_KEY and CODEX_API_KEY unless a task explicitly proves an intentional fallback path.
Keep the HTTP proxy private/internal and do not change apps/agent in this task.
Implemented OpenAI-compatible request/chunk/stream schemas, CodexRequestMapper, CodexStreamMapper, CodexDirectProvider, OpenAICompatibleProxy, safe proxy errors, live layers, mock layers, and tests.
No apps/agent or apps/codex-proxy files were changed. The deployed HTTP proxy remains the next task.
mandatoryVerification: Flat meaningful Effect.gen programs for proxy handling, Codex HTTP calls, request mapping, stream mapping, and token resolution.
Typed errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError.
Canonical Effect Schema contracts and schema-derived types from @bundjil/codex-oauth.
No unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, or trivial wrappers/helpers.
Tests cover OpenAI-compatible request decode, Codex Responses payload encode, streaming chunk mapping, proxy auth failure, HTTP status failure mapping, and safe error redaction.
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth test
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth build
bun run check-types
completionCriteria: @bundjil/codex-oauth exports direct provider/proxy schemas, errors, service tags, and memory/mock layers.
Package README documents direct Codex Responses scope, auth strategy, proxy security, and proof commands.
No Eve app model change is introduced.

evidence: Pass 1 - ownership/call graph: @bundjil/codex-oauth owns the package-level contract OpenAICompatibleProxy -> CodexDirectProvider -> CodexOAuthService/CodexRequestMapper/CodexHttpClient/CodexStreamMapper. apps/agent and apps/codex-proxy were not changed.

Pass 2 - Effect implementation quality: services use Context.Service, Layer, Schema-derived inputs/outputs, redacted internal tokens and upstream stream bodies, flat Effect.gen operations, named Effect.fn operations, and safe tagged errors. Subscription proxy mode does not read OPENAI_API_KEY or CODEX_API_KEY.

Pass 3 - verification coverage: tests cover OpenAI-compatible request decode, Codex Responses payload encode, stream chunk mapping, proxy auth failure, upstream HTTP status failure redaction, and no OPENAI_API_KEY fallback. Package test, build, root check-types, and root `bun run verification` passed before acceptance.

tasks.title: Create apps/codex-proxy Effect HTTP app
tasks.description: Add a deployable proxy app that composes @bundjil/codex-oauth services into Effect HTTP routes and exposes a private OpenAI-compatible streaming endpoint.
implementationNotes: Create apps/codex-proxy as a separate Bun workspace app with README.md, package.json, tsconfig.json, local dev entrypoint, Vercel fetch entrypoint, smoke-test scripts, and tests.
Mirror the Mobius apps/api architecture: app entrypoints own Vercel/Nitro/local dev; package code owns reusable Effect services and route behavior.
Use Effect HTTP platform APIs: typed JSON management routes through effect/unstable/httpapi and raw streaming /v1/chat/completions through effect/unstable/http/HttpRouter.
Use HttpRouter.toWebHandler at the app boundary and direct Request/Response tests for the handler.
Start with Bun or Node Vercel Function deployment, not Vercel Edge Runtime.
Implement health, internal bearer-token auth, mock-mode streaming, and private /v1/chat/completions route before any live Codex dependency.
Config belongs in apps/codex-proxy/src/env.ts and must use Effect Config with Config.redacted for BUNDJIL_CODEX_PROXY_INTERNAL_TOKEN.

Delegated worker 019f3d51-aea6-7de3-88c6-8b98eea158d6 was assigned this exact task but stayed running after two waits and produced no worktree changes; the parent closed it and implemented the slice locally.

Implemented apps/codex-proxy with package metadata, README, Effect Config service, app-owned route/config schemas and tagged errors, app-owned mock CodexDirectProvider layer, Effect HttpRouter routes, HttpRouter.toWebHandler boundary, Vercel-compatible one-argument fetch wrapper, local Bun dev server, smoke-test script, app-local Vitest config, and direct Request/Response tests.
The app composes @bundjil/codex-oauth OpenAICompatibleProxy and CodexDirectProvider service tags rather than duplicating Codex request mapping or stream mapping.
Live mode intentionally returns 503 in this slice; no Eve app model change and no Codex network call were introduced.
mandatoryVerification: Flat meaningful Effect.gen programs for route handlers, config parsing, auth checks, and proxy calls.
Typed errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError.
Canonical Effect Schema contracts and schema-derived types from @bundjil/codex-oauth and apps/codex-proxy route config.
No unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, or trivial wrappers/helpers.
Direct Request/Response tests cover GET /health, unauthenticated /v1/chat/completions rejection, invalid-token rejection, authenticated mock-mode streaming, and safe error redaction.
Smoke test proves local /health and mock-mode streaming with no Codex network call.
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy test
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy check-types
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy build
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy smoke-test
bun run check-types
completionCriteria: apps/codex-proxy exports a Vercel-compatible fetch handler and local dev host.
The proxy route is private by internal bearer token and can stream mocked OpenAI-compatible SSE.
apps/codex-proxy/README.md documents local dev, routes, env vars, Vercel project, smoke tests, and secret-handling rules.
No Eve app model change is introduced.

evidence: Pass 1 - ownership/call graph: apps/codex-proxy owns the deployable HTTP boundary, app config, Vercel fetch export, local dev host, route auth, mock streaming, smoke tests, and README. @bundjil/codex-oauth still owns reusable OpenAICompatibleProxy, CodexDirectProvider, schemas, and provider mapping. apps/agent was not changed.

Pass 2 - Effect implementation quality: route handlers use flat Effect.gen flow, Context.Service config, Layer composition, Effect Config with Config.redacted, Schema-derived route/config/package contracts, HttpRouter.toWebHandler, app-owned tagged errors, catchTags mapping, and package service tags. Parent scans found no OPENAI_API_KEY/CODEX_API_KEY reads and no committed token values.

Pass 3 - verification coverage: direct Request/Response tests cover GET /health, unauthenticated rejection, invalid-token rejection with redaction, and authenticated mock SSE. bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy check-types, test, build, and smoke-test passed before acceptance.

tasks.title: Deploy and verify codex proxy on Vercel
tasks.description: Link apps/codex-proxy to the personal Vercel project, deploy preview first, run direct HTTP verification, and only then approve production deployment.
implementationNotes: Link the app to a Vercel project named bundjil-codex-proxy in Cooper's personal Vercel account, not the Tilt Legal team.
Use preview deployment as the first hosted proof. Production deployment is allowed only after preview verification is recorded.
Set BUNDJIL_CODEX_PROXY_INTERNAL_TOKEN and other secrets through Vercel env vars; never commit .env files.
Hosted live Codex proof is opt-in. If credentials are unavailable, verify health/auth/mock streaming and record live proof as pending.
Use Vercel logs only for sanitized status and chunk-shape proof; do not log token values, authorization codes, raw OAuth responses, prompts, or full model output.

Added app-owned Vercel deployment files: api/index.ts, src/vercel.ts, and apps/codex-proxy/vercel.json. The Vercel function routes rewrites back to the existing Effect web handler without changing apps/agent or introducing live Codex network calls.

Linked project metadata confirms bundjil-codex-proxy belongs to Cooper Corbett's projects (team_1LX7ZujbijowTv8J9k0aU7nD), not Tilt Legal (team_G8r6j3RIfXPtqb3j71bNQMbO). Project settings use root directory apps/codex-proxy, framework Other, Node.js 24.x, output directory ., and build command bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy build.

Preview env vars are set in Vercel as encrypted Preview variables: BUNDJIL_CODEX_PROXY_INTERNAL_TOKEN and BUNDJIL_CODEX_PROXY_MODE=mock. vercel env pull succeeded to ignored apps/codex-proxy/.env.preview.local, but sensitive values were not printed.

Vercel SSO deployment protection was disabled for this proxy project so direct preview HTTP checks can reach app routes. /v1/chat/completions remains protected by BUNDJIL_CODEX_PROXY_INTERNAL_TOKEN. This exception applies only to the current mock-mode preview; live Codex mode or production must re-enable Vercel protection or provide an equivalent private network/control boundary.
Preview deployment dpl_38aC4YSWLkCESQiJATs5JqHsSn4X is READY at https://bundjil-codex-proxy-llqa9rwss-cooper-corbetts-projects.vercel.app with target preview.

Direct preview HTTP checks returned healthStatus 200, healthMode mock, unauthenticatedStatus 401, invalidTokenStatus 401, streamStatus 200, streamContentType text/event-stream, streamDataLines 2, streamDone true, and secret-leak booleans false.

Vercel request logs contained 4 lines and the sanitized pattern scan was clean for bearer values, OAuth/token terms, probe text, invalid-token text, and full mock response text. The deploy/env CLI logs were checked for the generated internal token value and were clean.
Production deployment was skipped. Hosted live Codex proof remains pending and opt-in.
mandatoryVerification: Flat meaningful Effect.gen programs remain intact after deployment-specific changes.
Typed errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError.
Canonical Effect Schema contracts and schema-derived types from owning packages are reused.
No unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, or trivial wrappers/helpers.
vercel link output or project metadata confirms bundjil-codex-proxy is under Cooper's personal Vercel account.
vercel env pull succeeds for preview and writes only ignored env files.
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy build
Preview deploy succeeds.
Preview direct HTTP GET /health returns 200 and no secret data.
Preview direct HTTP unauthenticated /v1/chat/completions returns 401 or 403.
Preview direct HTTP invalid-token /v1/chat/completions returns 401 or 403.
Preview authenticated mock-mode /v1/chat/completions streams OpenAI-compatible SSE.
Vercel logs and CLI output are checked for absence of access tokens, refresh tokens, authorization codes, raw OAuth responses, private prompts, and full upstream response bodies.
Production deploy is skipped or completed only after preview proof is recorded.
completionCriteria: Preview URL and sanitized verification evidence are recorded in the task ledger.
Production URL is recorded only if production deploy was completed.
Rollback instructions are documented in apps/codex-proxy/README.md.
No provider secret is committed or logged.

evidence: Pass 1 - ownership/call graph: apps/codex-proxy owns the Vercel api/index.ts entrypoint, rewrite adapter, vercel.json, project root settings, encrypted preview env vars, and hosted proof. @bundjil/codex-oauth service contracts remain unchanged, and apps/agent was not touched.

Pass 2 - Effect implementation quality: deployment-specific code is thin app-owned framework glue that delegates to the existing Effect HttpRouter.toWebHandler boundary. Existing flat Effect.gen route/config flow, Schema-derived contracts, Config.redacted secret parsing, and typed catchTags error mapping remain intact; no live Codex calls, DTO mirrors, unsafe casts, or API-key fallback were introduced.

Pass 3 - verification coverage: project/account metadata proved the personal Cooper Vercel scope, preview env vars were present without printing values, bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy check-types/test/build passed, preview deploy dpl_38aC4YSWLkCESQiJATs5JqHsSn4X reached READY, direct HTTP health/auth/mock-SSE checks passed, runtime logs and CLI output scans were clean, production was skipped, and README/spec/active-plan evidence was updated.

tasks.title: Wire Eve to the proven model provider
tasks.description: Add app-owned model-provider selection so apps/agent can choose AI Gateway fallback or the deployed private Codex proxy.
implementationNotes: Keep apps/agent/agent/config.ts as the app-owned config boundary.
Add app-owned model-provider wiring only after prove-codex-auth-runtime-path, create-codex-proxy-app, and deploy-codex-proxy-vercel complete or explicitly record a local-only exception.
Preserve the current Vercel AI Gateway model as a fallback until Codex proxy proof is stable.
Use @ai-sdk/openai-compatible only after the runtime endpoint is proven private and compatible.
The Eve app should call the proxy URL with BUNDJIL_CODEX_PROXY_INTERNAL_TOKEN and should not know about Codex OAuth internals.
Implemented apps/agent/agent/model-provider.ts with Effect Schema provider config for gateway and codex-proxy modes, plus @ai-sdk/openai-compatible LanguageModel construction for the private proxy.

Extended apps/agent/agent/config.ts to load provider mode, gateway model, proxy base URL, redacted internal token, optional proxy model, and context-window tokens through Effect Config and ConfigProvider.fromEnv().
Updated apps/agent/agent/agent.ts so Eve receives either the Gateway model string or the Codex proxy LanguageModel and receives modelContextWindowTokens only in codex-proxy mode.

Added apps/agent/test/model-provider.test.ts to prove Gateway is the default and Codex proxy mode sends bearer auth to /v1/chat/completions through injected fetch without placing the token in the request body.

Replaced manual JSON boundary handling in app/package request bodies, SSE chunks, smoke output, proof output, and leak checks with Effect Schema JSON encoders such as Schema.fromJsonString(...) and Schema.UnknownFromJsonString.
Local Gateway proof returned /eve/v1/info with model google/gemini-2.5-flash, Gateway endpoint connected, workspace_status discovered, and diagnosticCount 0.

Local Codex proxy proof ran apps/codex-proxy in mock mode on port 8788 and Eve in codex-proxy mode on port 2101. /eve/v1/info reported bundjil-codex-proxy/codex-default-model and contextWindowTokens 123456.

Local Codex proxy session proof emitted session.started, message.appended, message.completed, step.completed, turn.completed, and session.waiting with the mock proxy response and no token values in output.
mandatoryVerification: Eve agent model remains an AI SDK-compatible LanguageModel or Gateway model string.
Flat meaningful Effect.gen programs for provider selection and config parsing where fallible.
Typed errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError.
Canonical Effect Schema contracts and schema-derived types from owning packages.
No unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, or trivial wrappers/helpers.
bun run --filter @bundjil/agent test
bun run --filter @bundjil/agent build
bun run check-types
Local /eve/v1/info verifies the selected model/provider metadata without exposing secrets.
If the Codex proxy mode is enabled, local Eve session proof uses the private proxy and logs sanitized status only.
completionCriteria: apps/agent can select AI Gateway fallback or Codex proxy through Effect Config.
No provider secret is committed or logged.
App README documents the provider selection, proxy env vars, and fallback behavior.

evidence: Pass 1 - ownership/call graph: apps/agent owns provider config, provider selection, Eve modelContextWindowTokens wiring, app-local proxy env schemas, and app-level tests. @bundjil/codex-oauth owns OpenAI-compatible proxy contracts. apps/codex-proxy owns the HTTP proxy runtime. The Eve app does not import @bundjil/codex-oauth, CodexOAuthService, CodexProfileStore, direct Codex HTTP clients, hosted storage, or token refresh.

Pass 2 - Effect implementation quality: provider config uses Effect Config, ConfigProvider.fromEnv, Config.url, Config.redacted, Effect Schema literals/unions, Schema.RedactedFromValue, Match, Effect.all, flat Effect.gen, Effect.fn, withSpan, and Data.TaggedError mapping. JSON boundary encoding uses Schema.fromJsonString and Schema.UnknownFromJsonString. Parent scans found no unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual object readers, process.env reads in package logic, or API-key fallback.

Pass 3 - verification coverage: bun run --filter @bundjil/agent check-types/test/build passed; bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth check-types/test/build passed; bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy check-types/test/build/smoke-test passed; local Eve Gateway /eve/v1/info proof passed; local Eve Codex proxy /eve/v1/info and session stream proof passed; raw serialization scan across source/docs passed; bun run verification passed; bun run build passed.

tasks.title: Add a Vercel-compatible KeyValueStore adapter
tasks.description: Add an adapter for a Vercel Marketplace KV provider, expected to be Upstash Redis, behind Effect KeyValueStore.
implementationNotes: Do not use @vercel/kv; Vercel KV is sunset for new work.
Prefer Upstash Redis through Vercel Marketplace so env vars are provisioned through the linked project.
Keep the adapter behind KeyValueStore so @bundjil/codex-oauth does not depend on app code.
Decide and document token encryption before storing refresh tokens in hosted KV.

Delegated worker `019f3e29-854a-7a92-9ae0-e503fd287885` was assigned this exact task but timed out and was closed without a final response; parent reviewed, refined, verified, and accepted the resulting worktree changes.
Added `@upstash/redis` to @bundjil/codex-oauth and exposed `@bundjil/codex-oauth/upstash-key-value-store.layer` for opt-in hosted storage wiring.
Added canonical `UpstashRedisConfig`, `UpstashRedisRestUrl`, `UpstashRedisRestToken`, and `UpstashRedisKeyPrefix` schemas plus `UpstashKeyValueStoreConfigError`.
Implemented `loadUpstashRedisConfig`, `loadUpstashRedisConfigFromEnv`, `makeUpstashKeyValueStore`, and `UpstashKeyValueStoreLive` behind Effect KeyValueStore.

Config reads `UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL` / `UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN` with `KV_REST_API_URL` / `KV_REST_API_TOKEN` aliases; secrets use `Config.redacted`; live construction uses `new Redis(...)` rather than `Redis.fromEnv()` or direct package `process.env` reads.

The adapter uses `automaticDeserialization: false` so Effect Schema remains the JSON boundary and implements `clear` and `size` by scanning the configured Bundjil key prefix rather than operating on the whole Redis database.

Added mocked Upstash client tests for config loading, Vercel alias loading, missing-config tagged errors, KeyValueStore operations, prefix-scoped clear/size behavior, existing Codex profile store compatibility, and provider failure mapping.
Documented Vercel Marketplace / Upstash Redis provisioning, `vercel env pull`, env aliases, prefix behavior, and the hosted-token-storage blocker pending application-side envelope encryption.
mandatoryVerification: Flat meaningful Effect.gen programs for adapter operations.
Typed errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError.
Canonical Effect Schema contracts and schema-derived types from @bundjil/codex-oauth.
No unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, or trivial wrappers/helpers.
Tests use a mock or memory adapter by default; live Upstash tests are opt-in.

tasks.title: Make preview proof assertions enforce the live contract
tasks.description: Turn the preview proof's documented health, auth, streaming, and leak predicates into a typed success/failure contract so a failed authenticated request cannot exit successfully.
implementationNotes: Keep apps/codex-proxy/scripts/prove-preview.ts as the app-owned operator boundary.
Use Effect Schema for the proof result and tagged Effect failures for assertion errors; do not manually assemble JSON or use process.exit in domain helpers.
Keep one flat primary Effect.gen program. Do not add generic assertion or HTTP helper modules for this one proof.
Add deterministic coverage for a passing proof and at least one failed authenticated/SSE or leak predicate without provider credentials.

Implemented PreviewProofContract as the schema-owned success contract. The script emits status proved only after decode succeeds and emits only status blocked with non-zero exit for request, decoding, or assertion failure.

Added apps/codex-proxy/test/prove-preview.test.ts. It uses canonical health, error, and OpenAI-compatible chunk schemas for the fixture and proves both a sanitized success result and failed authenticated request.
